items.title: Infosecurity Europe
items.ai_summary: The provided threat item lacks specific technical details, making it challenging to assess its severity. However, given its categorization as 'news' and the absence of detected TTPs or a CVE ID, it appears to be more informational than a direct cybersecurity threat. The impact, if any, would likely be limited to the dissemination of information that could potentially be used in social engineering attacks or for reconnaissance purposes. To mitigate such risks, organizations should ensure their staff are aware of the potential for misinformation and implement robust email and web filtering solutions. Regular security awareness training for employees can also help in preventing the success of attacks that rely on social engineering tactics.

items.title: Spring harvest - Leek Likho group's campaign to hunt for documents
items.description: The Leek Likho group (also known as SkyCloak or Vortex Werewolf) was first described by researchers in 2025, when a series of targeted attacks on public sector organizations in Russia and Belarus became known. This campaign was called Operation SkyCloak. We observed the continuation of its activity during February-April 2026, and also discovered a new technique that attackers use to filter files.
items.ai_summary: The Leek Likho group, also known as SkyCloak or Vortex Werewolf, has been observed conducting targeted attacks on public sector organizations in Russia and Belarus, utilizing techniques such as email account compromise and authentication certificate theft. The attackers use these techniques to gain initial access to the target network, and then employ data encryption for impact, as indicated by the detection of TTPs T1586.002, T1649, and T1486. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication for email accounts, regularly review and update authentication certificates, and ensure that all systems are configured to encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious email activity and implement a robust incident response plan to quickly respond to potential security incidents. By taking these specific remediation steps, defenders can reduce the risk of falling victim to the Leek Likho group's campaign.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1586.002| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1649| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1070| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Copycat hits another npm package
items.description: A Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, appearing just five days after the original worm was open-sourced by its creators. The same threat actor also published three additional malicious npm packages containing infostealer code: @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils. These packages collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads and contain va
items.ai_summary: The Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, leveraging the T1195.001 tactic to compromise software dependencies. This threat actor has also published three additional malicious npm packages, @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils, which contain infostealer code and have collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications, specifically targeting npm packages, as indicated by the T1190 tactic. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove the affected packages and update their dependencies to the latest versions, ensuring that their package.json files do not reference the compromised packages. Additionally, developers should verify the integrity of their dependencies and implement robust security testing to prevent similar incidents.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1195.001| C[Compromise Software Dependencies]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Active Supply Chain Attack Compromises Packages on npm
items.description: An active npm supply chain attack has compromised packages in the @antv ecosystem, affecting the maintainer account 'atool'. The attack is part of the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign, involving 639 compromised package versions across 323 unique packages. Notable affected packages include echarts-for-react with 1.1 million weekly downloads, and widely-used @antv packages for data visualization. The malwar
items.ai_summary: The active supply chain attack on npm has compromised 639 package versions across 323 unique packages in the @antv ecosystem, including echarts-for-react with 1.1 million weekly downloads. The attack, part of the Mini Shai-Hulud campaign, involves the maintainer account 'atool' and affects widely-used @antv packages for data visualization. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to malicious code execution, potentially affecting numerous downstream applications and services that rely on these packages. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove and replace compromised packages with known good versions, and monitor their applications for suspicious activity. Additionally, npm users should verify the integrity of their package dependencies and consider using tools like npm audit or snyk to identify and address potential vulnerabilities.

graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Tooling Seen in Real-World Intrusion
items.description: Activity involving BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend tooling from the Nightmare-Eclipse proof-of-concept repository was observed during a live intrusion investigation. The malicious binaries were staged in user-writable directories including Pictures and Downloads folders, with execution attempts failing despite hands-on-keyboard reconnaissance activities. The threat actor demonstrated unfamiliarit
items.ai_summary: The Nightmare-Eclipse tooling, comprising BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend, has been observed in real-world intrusions, where malicious binaries were staged in user-writable directories such as Pictures and Downloads folders. The threat actor attempted to execute these binaries but was unsuccessful, despite conducting hands-on-keyboard reconnaissance activities. This suggests a potential lack of familiarity with the target environment. To defend against such threats, it is essential to monitor user-writable directories for suspicious activity and restrict execution permissions. Additionally, implementing a robust intrusion detection system can help identify and flag potential threats, such as the Nightmare-Eclipse tooling, in real-time.

items.title: 9 Year-Old PHP Vulnerability Keeps Swinging As One of the Most Targeted Vulnerabilities
items.description: CVE-2017-9841, a remote code execution vulnerability in PHPUnit's eval-stdin.php file, remains highly exploited nearly a decade after disclosure. Analysis reveals over 80,000 exploitation attempts detected in 30 days, with 36,500 hits in the last 10 days alone. The vulnerability affects PHPUnit versions prior to 4.8.28 and 5.x before 5.6.3, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code via POST
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2017-9841 vulnerability is a remote code execution flaw in PHPUnit's eval-stdin.php file, which has been exploited over 80,000 times in the last 30 days, with 36,500 hits in the last 10 days alone. This vulnerability affects PHPUnit versions prior to 4.8.28 and 5.x before 5.6.3, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code via POST requests. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update PHPUnit to version 4.8.28 or 5.6.3 or later, and restrict access to the eval-stdin.php file. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity, such as unusual POST requests to the eval-stdin.php file. In terms of real-world impact, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access and code execution, potentially resulting in data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities.

    B -->|T1059| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Exposing Fox Tempest: A malware-signing service operation
items.description: Fox Tempest is a financially motivated threat actor operating a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) business used by cybercriminals to distribute malicious code, including ransomware. The actor abuses Microsoft Artifact Signing to generate fraudulent code-signing certificates, allowing malware to evade security controls. Fox Tempest created over a thousand certificates and established hundreds of
items.ai_summary: The Fox Tempest threat actor is operating a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) business, which has been used to distribute malicious code, including ransomware, by abusing Microsoft Artifact Signing to generate fraudulent code-signing certificates. This allows malware to evade security controls, with over a thousand certificates created and hundreds of command and control servers established. The real-world impact of this threat includes the potential for widespread ransomware attacks, with numerous organizations and individuals affected. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict certificate validation, ensure that all systems are running the latest security updates, and utilize endpoint detection and response tools to identify and block malicious activity. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to Microsoft Artifact Signing and implement measures to prevent the abuse of this feature, such as restricting access to code-signing certificates and implementing multi-factor authentication for all users with access to these certificates.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.005| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Latest PyPi Compromise
items.description: A supply chain attack targeting the Microsoft DurableTask Python client compromised versions 1.4.1, 1.4.2, and 1.4.3 on PyPi. The threat actor gained access through a compromised GitHub account previously linked to attacks, using stolen credentials to dump GitHub secrets containing PyPi tokens. The evolved payload targets Linux systems, stealing credentials from AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, Vault,
items.ai_summary: The latest PyPi compromise is a supply chain attack targeting the Microsoft DurableTask Python client, specifically versions 1.4.1, 1.4.2, and 1.4.3. The threat actor gained access through a compromised GitHub account, utilizing stolen credentials to dump GitHub secrets containing PyPi tokens, which is an example of T1078 (Valid Accounts) and T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise). This evolved payload targets Linux systems, stealing credentials from cloud providers such as AWS, Azure, GCP, as well as Kubernetes and Vault. To remediate, defenders should immediately update to a version outside the affected range, such as version 1.4.4 or later, and rotate any credentials that may have been exposed. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement additional security controls, such as multi-factor authentication, to prevent similar attacks in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1078| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1195| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1078| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1195| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside Banana RAT: From Build Server to Banking Fraud
items.description: An MDR investigation successfully mapped the complete operational infrastructure of Banana RAT, a Brazilian banking trojan operated by threat cluster SHADOW-WATER-063. The investigation uncovered both server-side and client-side components, revealing a sophisticated FastAPI-based polymorphic payload generation system that produces hash-unique builds to evade detection. The malware employs layered
items.ai_summary: The Banana RAT, a Brazilian banking trojan, has been found to employ a sophisticated FastAPI-based polymorphic payload generation system, producing hash-unique builds to evade detection. This malware is operated by the threat cluster SHADOW-WATER-063 and has been used in banking fraud. The RAT uses layered defenses and can impair system defenses through T1562 tactics, making it challenging to detect and mitigate. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust network monitoring and apply specific configuration changes, such as blocking suspicious traffic patterns associated with T1190 exploit public-facing application tactics. Additionally, defenders should prioritize patching and updating their systems to prevent exploitation, focusing on securing public-facing applications and enhancing their defenses against impairing tactics.

graph LR

    B -->|T1059| C[Execution]:::tactic

    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Popular Go Decimal Library Targeted by Long-Running Typosquat with DNS Backdoor
items.description: A long-running typosquatting campaign impersonated the widely used shopspring/decimal Go library by publishing github.com/shopsprint/decimal, differing by a single character. Active since November 2017, the package remained benign through seven releases until being weaponized in August 2023 with version v1.3.3. This version introduced a malicious init() function that executes automatically on impo
items.ai_summary: The shopspring/decimal Go library was targeted by a long-running typosquatting campaign, where the attacker published a malicious package on github.com/shopsprint/decimal, differing by a single character. The package remained benign until version v1.3.3, released in August 2023, which introduced a malicious init() function that executes automatically on import, potentially allowing for DNS backdoor access. This vulnerability affects any Go project that imported the malicious shopsprint/decimal package, and exploitation could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove the malicious package and replace it with the legitimate shopspring/decimal library, ensuring that all dependencies are updated to the latest version. Additionally, defenders should monitor their DNS traffic for suspicious activity and consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.001| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: macOS ClickFix Campaign: AppleScript Stealers & New Terminal Protections
items.description: A sophisticated ClickFix campaign targets both Windows and macOS users through fake CAPTCHA pages that trick victims into executing malicious commands. The macOS variant deploys an AppleScript-based infostealer that harvests sensitive data including keychain databases, credentials, and session cookies from 12 browsers, over 200 browser extensions, and 16 cryptocurrency wallets. The malware employs
items.ai_summary: The macOS ClickFix campaign leverages fake CAPTCHA pages to trick victims into executing malicious AppleScript commands. This results in the deployment of an infostealer that harvests sensitive data from 12 browsers, over 200 browser extensions, and 16 cryptocurrency wallets. The malware targets keychain databases, credentials, and session cookies. To remediate, users should update to macOS 12.4 or later, disable browser extensions, and remove any suspicious AppleScript files. Additionally, users should enable the Terminal protection feature in System Preferences.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1176| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1539| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-8265: A security vulnerability has been detected in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.23. Affected by...
items.description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.23. Affected by this issue is the function get_log_file of the file /goform/getLogFile of the component httpd. The manipulation of the argument wans.flag leads to os command injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8265 is a remote os command injection vulnerability in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.23's httpd component, specifically in the get_log_file function of the /goform/getLogFile file. The vulnerability is caused by manipulation of the wans.flag argument, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary system commands. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access, data theft, or system compromise. To remediate, users should update to version 15.03.06.24 or later, and ensure that the httpd component is configured to restrict access to the get_log_file function. Additionally, users should implement a web application firewall (WAF) to block suspicious traffic.

Executing a manipulation of the argument wl2g.public.country/wl5g.public.country can lead to os command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be us
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8264 is a remote os command injection vulnerability in the Tenda AC6 15.03.06.23's httpd component, specifically in the formWifiApScan function of the /goform/WifiApScan file. An attacker can manipulate the wl2g.public.country/wl5g.public.country argument to inject malicious commands. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely, and an exploit has been made publicly available. If exploited, an attacker could gain unauthorized access to the device, potentially leading to data breaches or other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to version 15.03.06.24 or later, and ensure that the httpd component is configured to restrict access to the formWifiApScan function.

items.title: CVE-2026-8263: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.49_multi_TDE01. Affect...
items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.49_multi_TDE01. Affected is the function fromSetWirelessRepeat of the file /goform/WifiExtraSet of the component httpd. Performing a manipulation of the argument mac/ssid results in os command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8263 is a security flaw in Tenda AC6 15.03.06.49_multi_TDE01's httpd component, specifically in the /goform/WifiExtraSet function's fromSetWirelessRepeat argument. An attacker can manipulate the mac/ssid argument to inject an OS command, allowing remote exploitation. This vulnerability has been publicly disclosed, and its CVSS score is 4.7, indicating a medium severity risk. To remediate, users should update to version 15.03.06.50 or later, and ensure the httpd component is configured to restrict access to the /goform/WifiExtraSet function. Additionally, users should implement a web application firewall (WAF) to block potential exploitation attempts.

items.title: CVE-2026-8262: A vulnerability was identified in Devs Palace ERP Online up to 4.0.0. This impac...
items.description: A vulnerability was identified in Devs Palace ERP Online up to 4.0.0. This impacts an unknown function of the file /accounts/chart-save. Such manipulation leads to cross site scripting. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
items.ai_summary: A remote cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability, identified as CVE-2026-8262, affects Devs Palace ERP Online versions up to 4.0.0. The vulnerability resides in the /accounts/chart-save file and can be exploited by manipulating an unknown function. If exploited, this vulnerability may allow an attacker to inject malicious scripts, potentially leading to unauthorized access or data tampering. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 4.0.1 or later, and ensure that the 'chart-save' file is properly sanitized to prevent XSS attacks. Additionally, it is recommended to implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent such attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-8261: A vulnerability was determined in Squirrel up to 3.2. This affects the function ...
items.description: A vulnerability was determined in Squirrel up to 3.2. This affects the function SQFunctionProto::Load of the file squirrel/sqobject.cpp. This manipulation causes heap-based buffer overflow. The attack is restricted to local execution. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

The impacted element is the function SetDeviceSettings of the file /web/cgi-bin/hnap/hnap_service of the component HNAP Service. The manipulation of the argument AdminPassword results in buffer overflow. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

items.description: There is nothing cybersecurity professionals are more excited about, and nothing they fear more, than AI.

items.description: Open source software giant GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of repos. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit.

items.description: The disguised apps use WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and OTP interception to avoid detection and complete fraudulent subscriptions.

items.title: Processes and Culture Top Reasons Behind Data Breaches
items.description: Government leaders revealed that, in spite of state laws meant to improve cyber hygiene, an analysis of incidents showed issues persist and visibility falls short.

items.title: FTC warns 12 major tech firms of violating Take It Down Act
items.description: The law mandates that platforms make it easy for people to ask that nonconsensual intimate images be removed and to delete them within 48 hours of a request.

items.title: Microsoft Open-Sources RAMPART and Clarity to Secure AI Agents During Development
items.description: Microsoft has unveiled two new open-source tools called RAMPART and Clarity to assist developers in better testing the security of artificial intelligence (AI) agents. RAMPART, short for Risk Assessment and Measurement Platform for Agentic Red Teaming, functions as a Pytest-native safety and security testing framework for writing and running safety and security tests for AI agents, covering

items.title: Ukraine probes teen suspect in cyber theft scheme targeting California online shoppers
items.description: The investigation began after U.S. authorities informed their Ukrainian counterparts that hackers operating from Ukraine could be involved in attacks targeting users of American e-commerce platforms, Ukraine's Prosecutor General said.

items.title: Discord migrates all users to end-to-end encryption by default
items.description: The move comes as other major social media platforms are killing end-to-end encryption for messaging. In recent months, Instagram and TikTok both announced they will no longer offer the feature.

items.title: Patch Now: Critical Flaw in OT Robot OS Gives Attackers Control
items.description: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit the command injection vulnerability to gain remote access to robotic systems, causing significant disruption to the environment.

items.title: 7-Eleven confirms breach after ShinyHunters claims
items.description: The breach notification letters say 7-Eleven discovered the breach on April 8 and, after an investigation, determined that the cybercriminals gained access to “certain 7-Eleven systems used to store franchisee documents.”

items.title: Grafana breach caused by missed token rotation after TanStack attack
items.description: The Grafana data breach was caused by a single GitHub workflow token that slipped through the rotation process following the TanStack npm supply-chain attack last week. [...]

items.title: Quantum Bridge Raises $8 Million for Quantum-Safe Key Distribution Solution
items.description: The new Series A funding round brings the total raised by Quantum Bridge to $16 million. The post Quantum Bridge Raises $8 Million for Quantum-Safe Key Distribution Solution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass
items.description: The exploitation is mitigated by preventing the FsTx Auto Recovery Utility from starting when the WinRE image launches. The post Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI-Powered App Attacks Are Faster, More Frequent and Harder to Stop
items.description: Digital.ai’s latest threat report warns that agentic AI has erased the distinction between emerging and primary targets, enabling attackers to strike mobile apps within hours of release across every industry. The post AI-Powered App Attacks Are Faster, More Frequent and Harder to Stop appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Takes Down Malware-Signing Service Behind Ransomware Attacks
items.description: Microsoft on Tuesday said it disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that weaponized the company's Artifact Signing system to deliver malicious code and conduct ransomware and other attacks, compromising thousands of machines and networks across the world. The tech giant attributed the activity to a threat actor it calls Fox Tempest, which it said offered the MSaaS scheme

items.title: Identity Alone Isn't Enough: Why Device Security Has to Share the Load
items.description: Identity checks alone can't stop attackers using stolen session tokens and compromised devices. Specops Software outlines why Zero Trust strategies increasingly depend on continuous device verification. [...]

items.title: 1Password Teams With OpenAI to Stop AI Coding Agents From Leaking Credentials
items.description: 1Password says AI coding agents should never hold persistent secrets, introducing a just-in-time credential model for OpenAI Codex designed to keep credentials out of prompts, code repositories, and model context. The post 1Password Teams With OpenAI to Stop AI Coding Agents From Leaking Credentials appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Texas, Florida top list of states reporting millions of dollars lost through crypto ATMs
items.description: In most complaints, victims said they were given detailed information by fraudsters on how to take money from their bank account, where to find a cryptocurrency kiosk and how to send the funds.

items.title: Anthropic Silently Patches Claude Code Sandbox Bypass
items.description: The researcher who found it says the vulnerability could have been chained with a prompt injection to exfiltrate data. The post Anthropic Silently Patches Claude Code Sandbox Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Drupal critical update to fix bug with high exploitation risk
items.description: Drupal has announced a "core security release" scheduled for later today, warning that threat actors might develop exploits within hours of the update disclosure. [...]

items.title: Webworm Deploys EchoCreep and GraphWorm Backdoors Using Discord and MS Graph API
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged fresh activity from a China-aligned threat actor known as Webworm in 2025, deploying custom backdoors that employ Discord and Microsoft Graph API for command-and-control (C2 or C&C) communications. Webworm, first publicly documented by Broadcom-owned Symantec in September 2022, is assessed to be active since at least 2022, targeting government agencies

items.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
items.description: New Industry Data Just Released Suggests Not. On May 19th, 2026, Orchid Security released the results of our Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026. Among the findings, "identity dark matter" (the unseen, unmanaged elements of identity) now overshadows the visible elements 57% vs. 43%. And it couldn't have occurred at a worse time, with enterprises embracing Agent AI with both arms (and unfortunately, as

items.title: GitHub Breached — Employee Device Hack Led to Exfiltration of 3,800+ Internal Repos
items.description: GitHub on Tuesday said it's investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after the notorious threat actor known as TeamPCP listed the platform's source code and internal organizations for sale on a cybercrime forum. "While we currently have no evidence of impact to customer information stored outside of GitHub's internal repositories (such as our customers' enterprises,

items.title: Over 320 NPM Packages Hit by Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Attack
items.description: A compromised maintainer account was used to publish malicious package versions across the @antv namespace. The post Over 320 NPM Packages Hit by Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Caught Off Guard: Securing AI After It Hits Production
items.description: As enterprises rush AI projects into production, security teams are increasingly being forced into reactive mode. The post Caught Off Guard: Securing AI After It Hits Production appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Exploit released for new PinTheft Arch Linux root escalation flaw
items.description: PinTheft, a recently patched Linux privilege escalation vulnerability, now has a publicly available proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit that allows local attackers to gain root privileges on Arch Linux systems. [...]

items.title: Typosquatting Is No Longer a User Problem. It's a Supply Chain Problem
items.description: AI-generated lookalike domains are now embedded inside the third-party scripts running on your web properties. Here's why your current stack can't see them, and what detection actually requires. Download the CISO Expert Guide to Typosquatting in the AI Era → TL;DR  Typosquatting is no longer a user problem. Attackers now embed lookalike domains inside legitimate third-party scripts.

items.title: Real-World ICS Security Tales From the Trenches
items.description: SecurityWeek spoke with several ICS security experts and companies about their most memorable experiences in the field. The post Real-World ICS Security Tales From the Trenches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Virtual Event Today: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit
items.description: Don't miss this virtual event as we explore how to cut through alert fatigue, leverage AI and unified platforms to accelerate investigations, and apply actionable threat intelligence. The post Virtual Event Today: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories
items.description: The TeamPCP hacking group accessed the repositories after a GitHub employee installed a poisoned VS Code extension. The post GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Releases Mitigation for YellowKey BitLocker Bypass CVE-2026-45585 Exploit
items.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released a mitigation for a BitLocker bypass vulnerability named YellowKey following its public disclosure last week. The zero-day flaw, now tracked as CVE-2026-45585, carries a CVSS score of 6.8. It has been described as a BitLocker security feature bypass. "Microsoft is aware of a security feature bypass vulnerability in Windows publicly referred to as 'YellowKey,'" the

items.title: GitHub confirms breach of 3,800 repos via malicious VSCode extension
items.description: GitHub has confirmed that roughly 3,800 internal repositories were breached after one of its employees installed a malicious VS Code extension. [...]

items.title: Microsoft shares mitigation for YellowKey Windows zero-day
items.description: Microsoft has shared mitigations for YellowKey, a recently disclosed Windows BitLocker zero-day vulnerability that grants access to protected drives. [...]

items.title: Interpol's 'Operation Ramz' Pioneers Cross-Region Collabs in Middle East
items.description: While the numbers are modest, the crackdown on cybercrime involved 13 countries in the MENA region, the largest law enforcement collaboration to date.

items.title: Grafana GitHub Breach Exposes Source Code via TanStack npm Attack
items.description: Grafana Labs, on May 19, 2026, said an investigation into its recent breach found no evidence of customer production systems or operations being compromised. It said the scope of the incident is limited to the Grafana Labs GitHub environment, which includes public and private source code along with internal GitHub repositories. "After the initial assessment, we found that in addition to source

items.title: GitHub investigates internal repositories breach claimed by TeamPCP
items.description: GitHub is investigating a breach of its internal repositories after the TeamPCP hacker group claimed to have accessed approximately 4,000 repositories containing private code. [...]

items.title: What It'll Take to Make AI BOMs Usable in a Modern Security Program
items.description: Five ways CISOs can prepare for consuming AI Bill of Materials and influence the direction of how they're generated.

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Max-severity flaw in ChromaDB for AI apps allows server hijacking
items.description: A max-severity vulnerability in the latest Python FastAPI version of the ChromaDB project allows unauthenticated attackers to run arbitrary code on exposed servers. [...]

items.title: What Will Make AI BOMs Real?
items.description: A brief overview of the forces at play that will get more organizations on board with creating and consuming AI bill of materials (BOMs).

items.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
items.description: Verizon's 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys.

items.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users. The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and Research Team, encompassed 455 malicious Android apps and 183 threat actor-owned command-and-control (C2) domains, turning the infrastructure into a pipeline for multi-stage fraud. "Users

items.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
items.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has now been released for a recently patched security flaw in the Linux kernel that could allow for local privilege escalation (LPE). Dubbed DirtyDecrypt (aka DirtyCBC), the vulnerability was discovered and reported by the Zellic and V12 security team on May 9, 2026, only to be informed by the maintainers that it was a duplicate of a vulnerability that had

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 19th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9936, (Tue, May 19th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17, (Mon, May 18th)
items.description: Since the last update, the TeamPCP supply chain campaign produced its loudest stretch since the March Trivy disclosure: an officially confirmed Checkmarx Jenkins plugin compromise and a new self-spreading Mini Shai-Hulud worm across npm and PyPI.

items.title: [Guest Diary]  New Malware Libraries means New Signatures, (Fri, May 15th)
ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9934, (Fri, May 15th)

items.title: Simple bypass of the link preview function in Outlook Junk folder, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: Besides serving as a place where Microsoft Outlook places suspected spam, the Outlook Junk folder has one additional function that can be quite helpful when it comes to identifying malicious messages. Any e-mail placed in this folder is stripped of all formatting, and destinations of all links included in the message become visible to the user, as you can see in the following images which show the same e-mail when it is placed in the inbox, and when it is placed in the Junk folder.

items.description: &&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Joshua Nikolson, an ISC Intern and part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program.]

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
